title: Your First Function
description: Learn to create reusable code blocks with functions - the key to organized, efficient programming.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: You've been using functions like plant() and harvest(). Now let's learn to create your own!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Wait, I can make my own functions? Like plant() and harvest()?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! Functions are reusable blocks of code. Instead of copying the same commands repeatedly, you package them in a function and call it whenever needed.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So functions are like... creating my own custom tools?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Perfect analogy! Let's create your first custom tool.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: To create a function, use the 'def' keyword followed by a name and parentheses, then a colon. This defines your function!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! The colon at the end tells Python you're about to write the function's body. Try creating a function called greet!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Good start! But a function definition isn't complete without a body—the code it executes.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: The function body is the code that runs when you call the function. It must be indented! Add indented code under your def line.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Indentation matters in Python, right? Like with loops?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! Python uses indentation to show what's inside the function. Add some farming commands inside your function!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! You've defined a complete function. Now let's use it!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: To use a function, you 'call' it by writing its name with parentheses. This executes all the code inside!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So I define it once with def, then call it as many times as I want?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! That's the power of reusability. Call your function and click play to see it work!

objectives.description: Execute your code to see your function run

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: It worked! My custom function executed its code!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Congratulations! You've created and called your first function. Functions are fundamental to organized code.

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: What if I want my function to be more flexible? Like plant different crops?

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Great question! That's what parameters are for. Let's learn about those next!
